evidence: Shane describes Calendly moving toward a multi-product, AI-powered relationship management effort that will ship a CRM plus an "AI Executive System" in July for people who sell their time and currently hack together Notion or free HubSpot.
V offers to set up his mother-in-law (and sister-in-law) who are therapists with Calendly because bringing one modern system into an old-school practice is a massive opportunity to surface real pain.
V is positioning Careerspan for sale and is "super open" to product consulting as long as the team is taken care of and the exit is handled well.
Shane notes Calendly has been retooling the R&D team away from enterprise toward SMBs as it prepares the SMB-focused AI executive system.
V describes meeting Stacey Abrams' head of digital and wants to mobilize individuals for pro-democracy content before the next election, potentially using Zo as the coordination tool.
V will introduce Shane to Andy Moat via the Whispered GTM leaders network and send the intake link Andy prefers because Andy's network feels like a good fit for Shane's profile.
Shane commits to trying to introduce V to Tope so they can explore product consulting or a senior role, even though Calendly just hired a couple of people.

source_display: Data is the only competitive edge
evidence: V argues that the only edge will be data as companies build EU-compliant walled gardens, no matter the surface-level product differences.
V frames careers as the indirect money-making opportunity for Zo users, with e-commerce being the direct way to help people make money.

source_display: Subscription model for job search tool
evidence: David asserts that if someone is looking for a job they should subscribe until they get the job, signaling a SaaS subscription model for the envisioned careers product.

source_display: Zo needs an economic incentive for adoption
evidence: V says Zo is too technical for some users and not technical enough for others; adding an economic incentive—direct e-commerce or a careers layer—is the growth lever.
V tells David he can "shoot his shot" with the Zo folks and make the case for himself on his own terms.
David characterizes 2026 as a "meeting of the year" in which the relationship with V shifts from advisory to friendship, and they are focused on exploring partnership opportunities.
